title: Material composition and method of producing a material composition with enhanced barrier properties
abstract: A material composition with enhanced barrier properties and a method of producing said material composition, the material composition including a porous substrate having a void fraction, the void fraction of the substrate being at least partially filled with an inorganic precipitate to thereby reduce the porosity and permeability of the substrate.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1 . A material composition with enhanced barrier properties; including: a porous substrate having a void fraction, the void fraction of the porous substrate being at least partially filled with an inorganic precipitate to thereby reduce the porosity and permeability of the porous substrate. 2. A material composition according to claim 1 , wherein the porous substrate is selected from paper, nanofibre paper, microfibre paper and their composites, foam, gels, fabrics and wood. 3. A material composition according to claim 1 , wherein the porous substrate is formed with cellulosic fibres or cellulosic nanocrystals having a diameter range from 1 nm to 30 Î¼Î¹Î·. 4. A material composition according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the inorganic precipitate is formed from at least one salt solution selected from metal halides, metal carbonates, metal hydrogen carbonates, metal hydroxides and metal nitrates. 5. A material composition according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the inorganic precipitate is formed from a combination of salt solutions selected from metal halides, metal carbonates, metal hydrogen carbonates, metal hydroxides and metal nitrates. 6. A material composition according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the inorganic precipitate is formed from a combination of a gas and a salt solution selected from metal halides, metal carbonates, metal hydrogen carbonates, metal hydroxides and metal nitrates. 7. A material composition according to any one of claims 4 to 6, wherein the salt solution is selected from CaCI 2 , NaCO 3 , Ca(HCO 3 )2, Ca(OH) 2 , and Ag (NO) 3 . 8. A material composition according to claim 6, wherein the gas is CO 2 . 9. A material composition according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the inorganic precipitate is CaCO3. 10. A material composition according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the void fraction of the porous substrate includes pores having a size ranging from 1 nm to 10Î¼Î¹Î· . 1 1 . A material composition according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the porous substrate is formed using roll to roll technology. 12. A material composition according to any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the porous substrate is formed by filtration of nanofibre suspensions. 13. A material composition according to any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the porous substrate is formed by spraying or coating a nanofibre suspension onto another substrate. 14. A material composition according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the porous substrate has a thickness ranging from 1 Î¼Î¹Î· to 1 cm. 15. A material composition according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the porous substrate is flexible having an elastic modulus (E) less than 100 MPa. 16. A material composition according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the material composition has an oxygen permeability lower than 3 cc/m 2 day or 1 .2 cc^m/(m 2 .day.kPa) and a water vapour permeability lower than 5 gm/m 2 day or 1 x10 "11 g/(m.s.Pa). 17. A material composition according to claim 16, wherein the material composition has a basic weight ranging from 10 to 200 g/m 2 . 18. A material composition according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the material composition is recyclable by soaking and mechanical disintegration thereof. 19. A method of producing a material composition with enhanced barrier properties; including: a) exposing a porous substrate having a void fraction to at least one salt solution; and b) allowing the salt solution to precipitate and at least partially fill the void fraction of the porous substrate with an inorganic precipitate to thereby reduce the porosity and permeability of the porous substrate. 20. A method according to claim 19, including: exposing the porous substrate with a first salt solution, and then a second salt solution or gas, the first and second salt solution or gas reacting together to form the inorganic precipitate. 21 . A method according to claim 19 or 20, including: exposing the porous substrate to the at least one salt solution by dipping the porous substrate within the salt solution, coating the porous substrate with the salt solution, surface sizing the porous substrate with the salt solution, or spraying the salt solution onto the porous substrate. 22. A method according to claim 21 , wherein the salt solution is simultaneously or sequentially applied to at least one side of the porous substrate. 23. A method according to any one of claims 19 to 22, wherein the salt solution is selected from metal halides, metal carbonates, metal hydrogen carbonates, metal hydroxides and metal nitrates. 24. A method according to any one of claims 19 to 22, when the salt solution is selected from CaCI 2 , NaCO 3 , Ca(HCO 3 )2, Ca(OH) 2 , and Ag (NO) 3 . 25. A method according to claim 20, wherein the gas is CO2. 26. A method according to claim 24, wherein the molarity of the salt solution is in the range of 0.05 and 0.5 M. 27. A method according to any one of claims 19 to 26, wherein the porous substrate is selected from paper, nanofibre paper, microfibre paper and their composites, foam, gels, fabrics and wood. 28. A method according to any one of claims 19 to 27, wherein the porous substrate is formed with cellulosic fibres or cellulosic nanocrystals having a diameter range from 1 nm to 30 Î¼Î¹Î·. 29. A method according to claim 19, wherein the inorganic precipitate is CaCO 3 . 30. A method according to claim 19, wherein the void fraction of the porous substrate includes pores having a size ranging from 1 nm to 10 Î¼Î¹Î· . 31 . A method according to claim 19, wherein the porous substrate is formed using roll to roll technology. 32. A method according to claim 19, wherein the porous substrate is formed by filtration of nanofibre suspensions. 33. A method according to claim 19, wherein the porous substrate has a thickness ranging from 1 Î¼Î¹Î· to 1 cm. 34. A method according to claim 19, wherein the porous substrate is flexible having an elastic modulus (E) less than 100 MPa. 35. A method according to claim 19, wherein the material composition has an oxygen permeability lower than 3 cc/m 2 day or 1 .2 cc^m/(m 2 .day.kPa) and a water vapour permeability lower than 5 gm/m 2 day or 1 x10 "11 g/(m.s.Pa). 36. A method according to claim 35, wherein the material composition has a basic weight ranging from 10 to 200 g/m 2 . 37. A method according to claim 19, wherein the material composition is recyclable by soaking and mechanical disintegration thereof. 38. A material composition produced according to the method as claimed in any one of claims 19 to 37.
